data.title: Closed tariff loophole may impact shoppers as the $800 exemption on imports is no longer valid, causing confusion.
data.description: The recent elimination of the "de minimis" exemption allowing packages valued under $800 to enter the U.S. without tariffs has caused widespread confusion. Many individuals and businesses are grappling with the implications of this change, as they now have to navigate the tariffs on all imported goods regardless of their value. The removal of this exemption has significantly impacted the import process, forcing shippers to reassess their shipping strategies and potentially leading to increased costs for consumers. This shift has heightened uncertainty and complexity surrounding international trade, with many stakeholders seeking clarity on the new regulations and how they will affect their operations.

data.title: US Senator Lummis lauds Commerce Dept. for posting GDP data on blockchain platforms, calling it a "historic move." Coinbase CEO Armstrong praises decision, credits Commerce Secretary Lutnick.
data.description: Supporters of cryptocurrency celebrated the decision by the U.S. government to post Gross Domestic Product (GDP) figures on multiple blockchain platforms. Senator Cynthia Lummis from Wyoming described the move by the Commerce Department as "historic," highlighting that it will solidify America's position as a leader in digital innovation and transparency. She expressed gratitude to President of the United States (@POTUS) and Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick for their guidance. Similarly, Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong lauded the decision, emphasizing the significance of integrating government data onto blockchain technology, attributing this development to Lutnick's leadership. This move is seen as a crucial step in utilizing blockchain for smart contracts and oracles. Armstrong expressed Coinbase's pride in supporting the Commerce Department in this endeavor. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Court rules against DOL withholding contractor demographic data, impacting transparency efforts and records spanning three presidencies.
data.description: Last week, a federal appeals court dealt a setback to the Labor Department by ruling against their efforts to keep workforce demographic data of federal government contractors confidential. This decision comes after a prolonged struggle spanning three presidential terms.

data.title: Pro-trade groups are strategizing against Trump's tariffs, preparing for more outreach to secure relief.
data.description: Advocates for international trade are not persuaded by President Donald Trump's fondness for tariffs, and are working on increasing their efforts to seek assistance.

data.title: Trump administration undoes Biden-era policies disliked by big banks; includes restrictions on late fees, overdraft charges.
data.description: For several months, the Trump administration has been focused on reversing policies from the Biden era that big banks strongly dislike. These policies include limitations on credit card late fees and overdraft charges.

data.title: American Rebel Holdings clears up confusion regarding an amended S-1 filing on Aug 28; confirms no new filing, just a June 3 amend and June 27 declaration.
data.description: Following the circulation of misinformation in August 28 Benzinga posts, the Company received numerous investor inquiries. It clarified that there has been no recent filing of a new S-1/A. The mentioned amendment was actually filed on June 3, 2025, with the referenced registration becoming effective on June 27, 2025.
